stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under core NLP/ML tags like 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The mention of 'safetensors' indicates modern, efficient model handling practices.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
Indicates the model is relevant for text-generation tasks.
Categorizes the model within the broader transformers and Llama ecosystem.

stories.title: Sony is killing all physical PlayStation game discs
stories.author: Dominic Preston
bullet_points: Sony is phasing out physical game discs for new PlayStation titles.
The transition to digital-only releases for new games begins in January 2028.
Existing games released prior to the cutoff date will remain available on disc.

stories.title: After spooking Trump into safety testing, Anthropic AI models get global release
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Anthropic's advanced AI models, Fable and Mythos, are receiving a global release.
The release follows the US lifting previous safety testing curbs on the models.
This signifies a major step in the commercial availability of cutting-edge AI technology.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Highlights the trending status of the DeepSeek-V4-Pro model on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text generation tasks, indicating its primary application.
Tags confirm its relevance within the transformers and deepseek_v4 ecosystem.

stories.title: Faster Gemma 4 on MLX with multi-token prediction
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Gemma 4 inference speed is significantly improved within Ollama 0.31 on Apple Silicon.
The performance boost is achieved through the implementation of Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) powered by MLX.
Coding agent performance saw up to a 90% speed increase, as validated by the Aider polyglot benchmark.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using its MLX engine.
Performance improvements include faster response times and reduced memory usage.
The updates also result in higher quality outputs from the language models.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA is introducing Nemotron 3 Ultra, a new large language model.
The model is specifically engineered for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is designed to support complex, long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these agents can run entirely on the user's local hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, leveraging local LLM execution.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
The integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, optimizing AI workloads on Macs.
This is presented as a preview, indicating a significant step toward faster local AI inference on Apple platforms.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing for privacy and control.
The tool leverages Ollama, positioning itself within the growing trend of running LLMs locally.

stories.title: Xbox testing disc-to-digital feature that digitizes a physical game collection
stories.author: Tom Warren
bullet_points: Microsoft is reportedly planning to transition Xbox to a digital-only model, mirroring Sony's move.
A key feature under development is a disc-to-digital conversion tool for existing physical game collections.
This feature aims to allow current Xbox owners to digitize their physical game libraries before the end of disc production.

stories.title: Sony is killing discs — and showing us why it’s a terrible idea
stories.author: Andrew Webster
bullet_points: Sony announced the discontinuation of physical PlayStation disc production starting in January 2028.
This shift mandates that all future PS5 game purchases must be made digitally.
The move is framed by the article as a significant threat to video game preservation.

stories.title: Sony will stop making physical copies of PlayStation games in 2028
stories.author: Scharon Harding
bullet_points: Sony plans to cease manufacturing physical copies of PlayStation games by 2028.
This signals a major industry shift towards fully digital distribution for console games.
The announcement highlights the ongoing trend of 'owning nothing' in modern digital media consumption.

stories.title: Reinforcement Learning with Metacognitive Feedback Elicits Faithful Uncertainty Expression in LLMs
stories.author: Arman Cohan
bullet_points: The research addresses a critical LLM weakness: the inability to accurately express uncertainty and avoid confident hallucinations.
It proposes using Reinforcement Learning integrated with Metacognitive Feedback to improve LLM self-monitoring capabilities.
The goal is to enhance the trustworthiness and reliability of Large Language Models by enabling them to recognize knowledge boundaries.

stories.title: AxDafny: Agentic Verified Code Generation in Dafny
stories.author: Leopoldo Sarra
bullet_points: Introduces AxDafny, a framework for agentic code generation in Dafny that simultaneously generates executable code and formal proof artifacts.
The system uses a verifier-guided repair mechanism to iteratively refine implementations, invariants, assertions, and termination arguments.
Presents LiveCodeBench-Pro-Dafny (LCB-Pro-Dafny), a new benchmark suite of 250 competition-style problems for formal verification evaluation.

stories.title: Surrogate Fidelity: When Can Open LLMs Explain Closed Ones?
stories.author: Nehal Bandi
bullet_points: Addresses the core challenge of mechanistic interpretability (MI) when dealing with proprietary, closed LLMs.
Proposes evaluating 'surrogate fidelity'—determining when open model measurements can accurately represent closed model behavior.
Investigates fidelity across prediction, attribution, and representation levels using API-compatible readouts like log-odds.

stories.title: Reinforcement Learning with Metacognitive Feedback Elicits Faithful Uncertainty Expression in LLMs
stories.author: Arman Cohan
bullet_points: The research addresses a critical weakness in Large Language Models (LLMs): their inability to accurately express uncertainty.
It proposes using Reinforcement Learning combined with Metacognitive Feedback to improve LLM self-monitoring.
The goal is to reduce issues like confident hallucination and better delineate knowledge boundaries, enhancing LLM trustworthiness.

stories.title: US Supreme Court just blew up EU-US Data Transfers
stories.author: noyb.eu via kngl
bullet_points: The US Supreme Court has issued a ruling impacting EU-US data transfer mechanisms.
This decision represents a significant legal and regulatory shift for international data flows between the EU and US.
The ruling has the potential to disrupt current data governance frameworks relied upon by multinational tech companies.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release introduces performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically benefiting users on Apple silicon.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated support for subagents within Claude Code.
The update also enables web search functionality directly within Claude Code.
This enhances the capabilities of AI agents by adding complex reasoning (subagents) and real-time information retrieval (web search).

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Comcast’s split could make or break Peacock
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Comcast is planning to split NBCUniversal, Peacock, and Sky from its core broadband and wireless businesses.
This corporate restructuring forces Peacock to operate independently without the financial backing of a larger entity.
The move raises significant questions about Peacock's future viability and success in the competitive streaming market.

stories.title: Sony is closing the PS3 and Vita digital stores
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: Sony is discontinuing the digital storefronts for the PlayStation 3 and Vita.
The PS3 digital store closure will begin in select Latin American markets, such as Mexico, in August.
This marks the end of digital distribution services for these older PlayStation consoles.

stories.title: Google built a great smart speaker, but Gemini isn’t ready for it
stories.author: Jennifer Pattison Tuohy
bullet_points: Smart speakers are struggling to find a compelling use case beyond basic functions.
AI was anticipated to provide the next major evolution for smart speaker technology.
Google's integration of Gemini into its smart speaker hardware is currently facing readiness challenges.

stories.title: NASA inspector general suggests Boeing's Starliner will now be a decade late
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: NASA's Inspector General suggests a significant delay for Boeing's Starliner spacecraft.
The certification timeline for Starliner may now extend to 2027.
This represents a substantial delay of approximately ten years from the original schedule.

stories.title: Reddit will require you to log in to use old.reddit.com
stories.author: Scharon Harding
bullet_points: Reddit is implementing a login requirement for accessing the legacy old.reddit.com interface.
The primary motivation for this change is to combat abusive scraping activities.
This move represents a shift in Reddit's access control and platform maintenance strategy.

stories.title: Introspective Coupling: Self-Explanation Training Tracks Behavioral Change Despite Fixed Supervision
stories.author: Belinda Z. Li
bullet_points: The research investigates how to train Language Models (LMs) to produce faithful self-explanations of their predictions.
The study uses counterfactual behavior on modified inputs as a form of supervision for the explanation training.
Findings suggest that LMs can track behavioral change even when trained on fixed, historical, or behaviorally similar model explanations.

stories.title: QVal: Cheaply Evaluating Dense Supervision Signals for Long-Horizon LLM Agents
stories.author: Matthias Bethge
bullet_points: The paper addresses the challenge of sparse rewards in long-horizon LLM agent tasks.
It proposes QVal, a method for cheaply evaluating dense supervision signals used to guide intermediate actions.
The research focuses on improving guidance beyond traditional outcome-only rewards by assessing intermediate step quality.

stories.title: When LLMs Read Tables Carelessly: Measuring and Reducing Data Referencing Errors
stories.author: Huzefa Rangwala
bullet_points: LLMs exhibit Data Referencing Errors (DREs) when processing tables, even when they understand the table structure.
DREs are critical because they undermine the correctness of intermediate reasoning steps, not just the final answer.
This research provides the first systematic, large-scale evaluation of these tabular data referencing errors.

stories.title: Freeform Preference Learning for Robotic Manipulation
stories.author: Chelsea Finn
bullet_points: Addresses the bottleneck of reward design in autonomous robotic manipulation.
Introduces Freeform Preference Learning (FPL) to improve policy learning from human input.
FPL allows annotators to provide nuanced feedback beyond simple binary comparisons of trajectories.

stories.title: AdaJEPA: An Adaptive Latent World Model
stories.author: Mengye Ren
bullet_points: Introduces AdaJEPA, an adaptive latent world model designed to overcome limitations in traditional latent world models.
The core innovation is performing test-time adaptation directly within the closed loop of Model Predictive Control (MPC).
This addresses the critical issue of planning failure due to inaccurate predictions or test-time distribution shift.

stories.title: FLORA: A deep learning approach to predict forest attributes from heterogeneous LiDAR data
stories.author: Cédric Vega
bullet_points: Introduces FLORA, a deep learning model designed to predict forest attributes.
The model addresses the challenge of using heterogeneous LiDAR data for national-scale forest monitoring.
Its relevance lies in improving the robustness of forest inventory estimates despite variations in sensor and acquisition conditions.

stories.title: TRIAGE: Role-Typed Credit Assignment for Agentic Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Alborz Geramifard
bullet_points: TRIAGE introduces a role-typed credit assignment framework for agentic reinforcement learning.
It addresses a limitation in standard GRPO where the final outcome provides an incomplete signal for action credit.
The method aims to prevent punishing useful exploration and reduce reinforcement of redundant actions in complex agentic systems.

stories.title: PolicyGuard: From Organizational Policies to Neuro-SymbolicCompliance Review Engines
stories.author: Amar Prakash Azad
bullet_points: PolicyGuard introduces a neuro-symbolic framework to address limitations in LLM-based compliance review.
The system aims to make policy logic explicit, allowing for easier inspection, updating, and testing of compliance decisions.
It specifically targets the challenge of determining if documents adhere to complex, organization-specific policies and guidelines.

stories.title: Introspective Coupling: Self-Explanation Training Tracks Behavioral Change Despite Fixed Supervision
stories.author: Belinda Z. Li
bullet_points: The research investigates whether training Language Models (LMs) to generate explanations leads to genuine 'faithful introspection' or just superficial imitation.
The study uses counterfactual behavior on modified inputs as a form of supervision for the LMs.
Surprisingly, the models show behavioral change even when trained on fixed counterfactual explanations derived from earlier or similar models.

stories.title: QVal: Cheaply Evaluating Dense Supervision Signals for Long-Horizon LLM Agents
stories.author: Matthias Bethge
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of sparse rewards in long-horizon LLM agent tasks.
Proposes QVal, a method for cheaply evaluating dense supervision signals used to guide intermediate actions.
Highlights the common pitfall of evaluating dense supervision methods solely based on downstream performance.

stories.title: Freeform Preference Learning for Robotic Manipulation
stories.author: Chelsea Finn
bullet_points: Addresses the critical bottleneck of reward design in autonomous robotic manipulation.
Introduces Freeform Preference Learning (FPL) to overcome limitations of binary preference labeling.
Allows human annotators to provide nuanced feedback on robot trajectories rather than simple 'better/worse' choices.

stories.title: AdaJEPA: An Adaptive Latent World Model
stories.author: Mengye Ren
bullet_points: Introduces AdaJEPA, an adaptive latent world model designed to improve planning capabilities.
Addresses the critical weakness of traditional latent world models: failure under test-time distribution shift due to frozen predictions.
Integrates test-time adaptation directly into the closed-loop Model Predictive Control (MPC) framework.

stories.title: Automated Background Swapping for Robustness against Spurious Backgrounds
stories.author: Kajetan Schweighofer
bullet_points: Addresses the critical issue of spurious correlations in Deep Neural Networks, where models rely on non-causal features.
Focuses specifically on the vision domain, where background elements often introduce these misleading correlations.
Proposes an 'Automated Background Swapping' method to improve classifier robustness against such spurious backgrounds.

stories.title: TRIAGE: Role-Typed Credit Assignment for Agentic Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Alborz Geramifard
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation in standard Reinforcement Learning (RL) where a single final outcome signal is used for credit assignment in agentic systems.
TRIAGE proposes a novel 'role-typed credit assignment framework' to better attribute success or failure to specific environment-facing actions (e.g., clicks, searches).
This method aims to solve issues where standard RL punishes useful exploration or reinforces redundant actions based solely on the final result.

stories.title: FedLAB: Traceable Semantic Codebooks for Federated Multimodal Graph Foundation Learning
stories.author: Guoren Wang
bullet_points: Introduces FedLAB, a method for federated multimodal graph foundation learning.
Addresses the challenge of training large models on decentralized, privacy-sensitive multimodal graph data.
Focuses on creating traceable semantic codebooks to enable knowledge sharing without centralizing raw data.

stories.title: CoMet: Context and Multiplicity Decomposition for Multimodal Uncertainty Estimation
stories.author: Olga Russakovsky
bullet_points: The paper addresses the critical, long-standing challenge of uncertainty estimation in AI models, particularly Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs).
It introduces 'CoMet,' a method designed for Context and Multiplicity Decomposition to better estimate uncertainty in complex multimodal systems.
The research highlights that uncertainty in MLLMs can arise from diverse sources and the relationships between them, making robust estimation crucial.

stories.title: Introspective Coupling: Self-Explanation Training Tracks Behavioral Change Despite Fixed Supervision
stories.author: Belinda Z. Li
bullet_points: The research investigates whether training Language Models (LMs) to generate self-explanations leads to genuine introspection or just superficial imitation.
The study uses counterfactual behavior on modified inputs as a form of supervision for the LMs' explanation generation.
Findings suggest that LMs can be trained effectively using fixed counterfactual explanations derived from past checkpoints or similar models.

stories.title: QVal: Cheaply Evaluating Dense Supervision Signals for Long-Horizon LLM Agents
stories.author: Matthias Bethge
bullet_points: The paper addresses the challenge of sparse rewards in long-horizon LLM agent tasks.
It focuses on improving dense supervision signals, which score intermediate actions rather than just final outcomes.
The research proposes a method (QVal) to evaluate these dense supervision signals more effectively than current downstream performance metrics.

stories.title: When LLMs Read Tables Carelessly: Measuring and Reducing Data Referencing Errors
stories.author: Huzefa Rangwala
bullet_points: LLMs exhibit data referencing errors (DREs) when processing tables, even if they understand the table structure.
DREs are critical because they compromise the correctness of intermediate reasoning steps, not just the final answer.
This work provides the first systematic, large-scale evaluation of these tabular data referencing errors.

stories.title: Generative Skill Composition for LLM Agents
stories.author: Tianlong Chen
bullet_points: The paper addresses the growing challenge of selecting appropriate 'skill compositions' for complex LLM agents.
Skills are defined as modular packages of procedural knowledge used by LLMs for specialized tasks (e.g., environment setup, testing).
Existing methods for skill composition selection are categorized, indicating a focus on improving agent orchestration.

stories.title: Scalable Behaviour Cloning on Browser Using via Skill Distillation
stories.author: Qinhuai Na
bullet_points: Proposes using human web browsing behavior as a scalable source of reusable skills for AI agents.
Identifies decision-making under incomplete information as the primary bottleneck for browser agents, not low-level operations.
Introduces a method, 'Skill Distillation,' to leverage implicit knowledge present in human interaction traces.

stories.title: DigitalCoach: Communication and Grounding Gaps in Human and Agentic Computer Use Coaching
stories.author: Amy Pavel
bullet_points: Introduces DigitalCoach, a multimodal dataset designed to study human-agent coaching in computer use.
The dataset comprises 72 expert-novice coaching sessions, including 22,752 dialogue turns grounded in screen and input event recordings.
The research aims to evaluate the capability of state-of-the-art AI models to effectively teach humans software usage.

stories.title: MECoBench: A Systematic Study of Multimodal Agent Collaboration in Embodied Environments
stories.author: Zhongyu Wei
bullet_points: Introduces MECoBench, a new benchmark for evaluating multimodal agent collaboration in embodied environments.
Addresses the gap in understanding how multimodal LLMs can effectively collaborate in visually grounded tasks.
Evaluates MLLMs across diverse tasks, cooperation structures, and collaboration modes, yielding key findings on collaboration.

stories.title: For first time, a cell built from scratch grows and divides
stories.author: defrost
bullet_points: Researchers have successfully created a cell from scratch that exhibits growth and division.
This represents a significant step in synthetic biology and cellular engineering.
The research is published/detailed on the Biotic.org platform.
